kjv_text: …they shake the head, saying, He trusted on the LORD that he would deliver him: let him deliver him…

thread.thread: Psalm 22:7-8, written ~1000 years early, records the mockers' GESTURE (shaking the head) and their TAUNT ('let him deliver him'); at the cross the crowd did exactly both — Matthew 27:39 wagging their heads and 27:43 sneering 'let him deliver him now' — the mockers unknowingly reciting the prophecy that marks Him as the One.
thread.lever: NT-confirmed OT echo (predictive correspondence, undisputed text both sides)
thread.anchor_detail: the crowd at the foot of the cross wagging and shaking their heads and sneering the taunt 'let him deliver him', the gesture and words David recorded a thousand years before
thread.why_fresh: the mockers, trying to discredit Him, unknowingly recited the very prophecy that identifies Him — the same gesture and the same taunt, from people who never saw what they were echoing
thread.gospel_landing: He wasn't delivered — not because He lacked the power to come down, but because staying on the cross was the only way to deliver you; weakness did not pin Him there, love did; come to Him
thread.rationale: an airtight, undisputed double match (gesture + taunt), with a voluntary-substitution landing that denies impotence

beats.text: At the cross they shook their heads and sneered the very taunt a psalm had recorded a thousand years before.
Psalm twenty-two. David, describing a mocked and dying man, even writes his tormentors' words: "…they shake the head, saying, He trusted on the LORD that he would deliver him: let him deliver him…"

A thousand years later, at the cross, Matthew records both — the passers-by wagging their heads, and the religious leaders jeering: "He trusted in God; let him deliver him now…" The same gesture. The same taunt — flung at Him by people who never saw what they were echoing.
"Let him deliver him," they sneered. And He wasn't delivered — not because Jesus lacked the power to come down; He had every power. He stayed because staying was the only way to deliver you.
They thought weakness pinned Him to that cross. It was love. He could have come down in an instant — He chose to stay, with you in view.
